prompt: A retro Japanese movie poster for a fictional anime film, designed like an overdramatic 1980s to 1990s theatrical poster with intentionally tacky but charming pulp energy. The scene shows a teenage girl manga artist in a dim wooden Japanese room at night, seated on the floor beside a low desk covered with scattered manuscript pages, pens, ink, books, and drawing tools. She has messy dark shoulder-length hair blown by supernatural force, a cream-beige long-sleeve blouse with loose ruffles, and a dark plaid skirt. Her face is obscured by a soft rectangular blur. She stretches one arm upward, holding a glowing smartphone toward a shattered window as a blinding explosion of light and lightning bursts through the glass, illuminating the whole room with gold-white energy. Outside the window is deep blue night with stormlike streaks and electric cracks. The composition is dynamic and diagonal, with the raised arm and device pointing into the central blast. Use painterly anime key art styling, dramatic rim light, textured film-poster shading, rich teal, navy, amber, and crimson colors, and a nostalgic hand-painted cinema-poster feel. Fill the poster with bold Japanese promotional copy in multiple blocks on the left side, including exaggerated red and yellow emphasis marks and three small cartoon reaction faces of a classic manga boy character. Add a vertical Japanese tagline on the right side. Place an enormous stylized Japanese title across the lower middle in red and blue metallic gradient lettering with sharp highlights and heavy shadow, reading トキヲ描ける少女. Include a cream starburst badge near the lower right with more Japanese text and a small manga face. At the lower left, add a large release date block in Japanese with bright yellow numerals and white text, defaulting to 2025年 7月18日(金). Along the bottom edge, add exactly 6 small rectangular film-strip preview stills from the movie. Add small production credits in Japanese and English across the bottom center, including Building Fictions. Make the whole layout feel like a fake time-travel comedy anime poster set in 1935, loud, nostalgic, cinematic, and slightly cheesy in the best possible way.
